changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Before the Flight"

TextENGNew: ``Here's your plane ticket. Don't be late.@k@vS20/00/badA44002. You'll get the rest of the money when we arrive.``
changeReason: Old says "when you get there" (implying only one person travels) while new says "when we arrive" (implying both travel together), changing the understood plan for the getaway.

TextENGNew: ``Kyahaha, thanks! I've always wanted to visit Sapporo!``
``The only things big about him are his voice and his attitude.``
changeReason: Old mistranslates 声 (voice) as a vulgar sexual term; Japanese clearly says 'voice and attitude.'

TextENGNew: At that moment, they heard the sharp screech of tires--several of them.
changeReason: Old omits the key sensory detail of tire screeching (タイヤの鋭い泣き声) and just says "cars coming," losing the urgency and threat conveyed by the sound.

TextENGNew: Startled, the man peered through a gap in the curtains. Three black cars had just screeched to a halt in front of the apartment building, parked at reckless angles.
changeReason: Old omits that he peered through a curtain gap (カーテンの隙間から) and the detail of reckless parking angles (乱暴な停め方), both of which convey the furtiveness and menace of the scene.

TextENGNew: A swarm of clearly dangerous-looking men poured out of the cars.
Several of them looked up toward this very window.
He thought their eyes met and hastily closed the sliver of curtain.
changeReason: Old omits the crucial detail that he felt their eyes met (目が合った気がして), which adds tension and the sense of being discovered to the scene.
Old says "they're here" (mere arrival) while new says "they found us" matching 嗅ぎ付けやがった (they sniffed us out), implying active tracking and discovery rather than coincidence.

TextENGNew: ``Wait... no way, seriously?! Why today of all days?!``
changeReason: Old omits "today of all days" (何で今日) which conveys that this was their last day before fleeing — critical context for understanding the dramatic irony of their escape being foiled.

TextENGNew: ``Move it, Mamiya!! If they catch us, we're dead!!!``
changeReason: Old addresses only "you" (Mamiya) while the Japanese uses inclusive "we'll be killed" (殺されンぞ); also old lacks the conditional "if caught" (捕まったら) present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: The thunder of many footsteps charging up the emergency stairs, accompanied by furious shouts, was closing in fast.
changeReason: Old omits "emergency stairs" (非常階段), reduces "furious shouts" (怒声) to merely "sounded angry," and loses the urgency of "closing in fast" (すぐそこまで迫ってきていた).
